Name: 1_passed away_died_jane_funeral
Representative_Docs: ['Janet Marie Flores Birth: 1931 Death: 2025 Janet Marie Flores passed away peacefully on November 21, 2025 at the age of 94 surrounded by the love of her family. Jan was born on October 31, 1931 in San Francisco to loving parents Alice Detour and Joseph Hettrick. Jan was a loving mother, grandmother and great grandmother who found her greatest joy in her family. She had a way of making people feel welcomed. She was kind, big hearted, extremely gracious and devoted to those she loved. Her presence will be deeply missed. Jan lived a long life traveling, playing golf, bunco with her friends and one of her greatest talents was to find a treasure at garage sales. In her final years she enjoyed playing penny slots at the casino. The last nine years Jan spent in the loving care of her daughter and son in law. A special thank you to her granddaughter Samantha for making her last days warm, comfortable and full of love. Jan survived by her daughter Tina Schilling (Michael), her grandchildren Michael (Marissa), Paul (Kacie), Samantha (Carlos), and her nine great grandchildren Jackson, Aubrey, Jordan, Mikey, Ellie, Luna, Ryder, Makayla and Stella. And her friends who will forever cherish her memory. Celebration', 'Paul (Paulie) Abato Birth: 1933 Death: 2025 Beloved brother, husband, father, grandfather, and great grandfather, Paul (Paulie) Abato passed away on Oct 23, 2025 at his home in Laguna Niguel, CA just shy of 92 yrs. Paulie was born to Jeanne and Natale Abato, was raised in Belleville, NJ, and was one of three children. Paulie was a proud meat-cutter for 38 years. He loved baseball, basketball, football, and boxing. He cherished many close friends, near and far, and was an original Jersey boy with Chickie, Frankie, and Tommy. He is survived by his two beautiful sisters, Raffaella (Paul) and Judith (Bill); three children, Janine (Steve), Nicholas, and Lisa (Greg); four grandchildren, Makena (Chris), Adam, Elizabeth, and Abigail; two sweet great-grandchildren, Carmina and Nora; and many nieces, nephews, and cousins. He was predeceased by his loving wife, Carmine, and oldest son, Joseph. Funeral mass will be held on Feb. 7 at 1 pm at St. Kilian Catholic Church. For those who wish to honor his memory, donations may be made to the American Heart Association or the Alzheimer’s Association. Honor with Flowers In memory of Paul Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Paul', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 2_family_friendship_nephew_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: We’ve had a rough year. How do we tell family we want no gifts this holiday? Dear Eric: In a year that has been filled with not-so-great things,( family members being furloughed, health issues, rising costs) how does one tell the family you don’t want any gifts? My husband and I have plenty of things, we don’t need any things or clothing or food. In the past the family members have all planned lists. There are children that will get gifts from Santa but how do we tell the rest of the fairly large family that enough is enough? – Realistic Gift-Giving Dear Gift-Giving: Meet their lists with a list of your own. You can make a list of charities you’d like them to contribute to in lieu of gifts, or a list of gifts that can’t be bought, like quality time or homemade treats. Or your list can have just one item: “we have everything we could want. All we ask for is your continued love and support.” Dear Eric: This is in response to “Walking Seniors,” whose neighbor would turn the sprinkler on them when they walked by his house. I happen to be a lawyer. I would suggest the letter writer bypass the police who are doing nothing, go directly to his state attorney or district attorney’s office and file a complaint of harassment. And I would get a temporary restraining or', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Representative_Docs: ["Dusk's £8 sale lamp that 'doesn't need cables' is 'so bright' without a plug The lamp has been reduced in price (Image: Dusk) A lamp can be the perfect finishing touch to a room - it can add a warm glow and illuminate any dark spaces while bringing style and colour. Luckily for shoppers, Dusk has reduced the price of one of its lamps, and shoppers say it is 'ideal for lighting up dark corners'. The Robin Reactive Glaze Ceramic Bulb Table Lamp in the colour 'Green/Brown' is currently available from Dusk for £8, down from £12. It also comes in the colour 'Cream' for £5, down from £12, and Dusk says it will 'add a touch of industrial chic' to any space. The Robin Reactive Glaze Ceramic Bulb Table Lamp's speckled glaze finish and exposed bulb create a raw, stylish look, Dusk says, and it is described as 'compact', measuring 9cm x 9cm x 15cm. The lamp is also battery-powered, making it perfect for bedside tables, desks, or any space that needs a little extra light. It is worth noting that three AAA batteries, which are not included, are needed to light the integrated LED bulb. However, there is no assembly required, meaning the lamp can be placed in the desired space and used straightaway. It's worth noting that pillowcases aren't included and must be purchased separately, starting from £15 for one, for example. Name: 4_cultural events_travel_world cup_olympics
Representative_Docs: ['Sporting And Cultural Events Boost Travel Intentions For 2026 Photo Credit: Deposit Photos Thirty-four percent of American travelers say they are likely to take an overnight trip to attend a sporting event in 2026. Twenty percent plan to attend a FIFA World Cup match, and 52% intend to travel overnight for cultural events such as concerts and festivals. Meanwhile, 47% are interested in touring Route 66 during its 100th anniversary year. These figures highlight a shift for the coming year where travel decisions are increasingly driven by events rather than relaxation. In 2026, Americans are choosing what to attend, not just where to go. The latest Longwoods International study shows that milestone celebrations and global competitions are now major motivators for travel. The calendar is driving demand. “Special events such as the FIFA World Cup, America 250 and the 100th anniversary of Route 66 will all motivate U.S. travel this year,” said Amir Eylon, President and CEO of Longwoods International. The industry has emphasized experience-based travel, but 2026 denotes a more specific trend. Events offer deadlines, shared participation, and reasons to plan trips in advance. The FIFA World Cup brings global visibility and international visitors. Name: 5_museum_egypt_egyptian_world largest
Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 6_immigration_britain_immigrants_diversity
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 7_strictly_contestants_itv_couples
Representative_Docs: ["Married At First Sight Australia has just exposed this couple's dealbreakers Homestays week has a habit of turning even Married At First Sight Australia's strongest relationships into something far more fragile. And this Wednesday's (28 April) episode proved Alissa and David were no exception. While the pair initially appeared to be one of this season's more promising couples, their latest test outside the experiment revealed that chemistry and emotional connection do not always guarantee long-term compatibility. Like many MAFS couples before them, Alissa and David entered the experiment with optimism, quickly emerging as one of the more stable pairings on the Channel 4 reality series, despite some awkward moments. But homestay week has done what it does best: force reality into the fantasy, bringing up difficult questions in tow. Related: Best streaming services Wednesday's episode saw David travel to Alissa's hometown of Adelaide, where their relationship faced one of its biggest turning points yet. Removed from the controlled environment of the experiment, the couple were confronted with real-life decisions about where they might actually live and what kind of future they truly wanted. Cracks began to emerge almost immediately. A discussion about whether", 'Strictly Come Dancing fans have same complaint as La Voix withdraws from show \'Pointless!\' Strictly Come Dancing fans have been left with the same question after it was announced La Voix has withdrawn from the competition. During Saturday (November 22) night\'s show, the remaining contestants took to the dance floor at the iconic Blackpool Tower Ballroom. Before things kicked off for Blackpool Week, presenter Tess Daly informed viewers that the drag queen – real name Chris Dennis – would not be returning to the competition after sustaining a foot injury. She said: "We can confirm she has very sadly had to withdraw from the competition," before revealing there would be no elimination this weekend. But this has left fans wondering why Sunday night\'s show is still airing now that there is no dance-off. Taking to social media with their queries, one wrote: "So what’s the point of Sunday’s show when everything can be done tonight #Strictly?" Read more: Strictly pro says only one person can replace Craig Revel Horwood Read more: BBC Strictly viewers switch off as major star pulls out of show We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you\'ve consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You', 'Strictly Come Dancing star breaks silence after concern fifth contestant would quit Strictly Come Dancing\'s Alex Kingston is poised to carry on with her dance adventure, putting to rest concerns she might have to withdraw due to injury. The 62-year-old actress had triggered concerns about a potential exit after her daughter disclosed a rib injury on social media, stating that her mother had endured the discomfort to participate in Saturday\'s programme. The Doctor Who legend, who has been dazzling audiences on the dance floor alongside partner Johannes Radebe, was swift to comfort her supporters that injuries were simply "part and parcel" of the Strictly experience, and she had absolutely no plans to retire her dancing shoes. Fleur East, host of Strictly spin-off It Takes Two, has now verified that Alex will return to the competition following last week\'s Blackpool special. Discussing their forthcoming performance, Fleur disclosed: "Alex and Johannes will be dancing a cha-cha-cha to Ring My Bell by Anita Ward." Name: 8_new season_episodes_hbo_emmy
Representative_Docs: ['Is There a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 Release Date & Time? Viewers are wondering if there’s a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 release date and time. The popular drama series has recently released its 10th episode, leaving fans eager to know whether the season is over. Here’s all you need to know. There isn’t a The Morning Show Season 4 Episode 11 release date and time. This is because the season has ended with just 10 episodes, according to the show’s official schedule. The Morning Show is a drama series that delves into the inner workings of a high-profile network broadcast morning news program. The series features a stellar cast that includes Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, and Billy Crudup, among others. Yes, The Morning Show Season 4 has finished. The fourth season of the show released its 10th and final episode, titled “Knowing Violation,” recently. There won’t be an additional episode in the coming weeks. However, fans need not worry as it will come back with a new season. In September 2025, Apple TV+ announced that The Morning Show had been renewed for Season 5. But a release window has yet to be confirmed. You can watch The Morning Show Season 4 via Apple TV+. Name: 9_cancer screening_cardiovascular_kidney_new study
Representative_Docs: ["Mammograms may reveal hidden heart risks for women, Penn State study finds Routine mammograms are best known as a front-line tool for detecting breast cancer. But new research suggests the same X-ray images may also offer an early warning sign for cardiovascular disease — the leading cause of death among women. A study presented at a Dec. 3 Radiological Society of North America meeting found that calcium deposits visible in the arteries of the breast can help predict a woman’s future risk of heart attack, heart failure, stroke and death. The findings add to growing evidence that mammograms may hold untapped value beyond cancer screening. Javascript is required for you to be able to read premium content. Signup today!", 'Disagreement Between Two Kidney Function Tests Predicts Serious Health Problems NEW YORK and HOUSTON, Nov. 7, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- A mismatch between two common tests for kidney function may indicate a higher risk for kidney failure, heart disease, and death, a new study shows. Health care providers for decades have measured blood levels of the molecule creatinine to track the rate at which kidneys filter waste from muscle breakdown in the bloodstream. According to more recent guidelines, levels of cystatin C, a small protein made by all cells in the body, can also be used to measure kidney function. Since these two tests are influenced by different factors — including some related to disease or aging — using both markers together can provide a better measure of kidney function and risk of organ failure than either one alone. Since these two tests are influenced by different factors — including some related to disease or aging — using bo', 'Disagreement Between Two Kidney Function Tests Predicts Serious Health Problems NEW YORK and HOUSTON, Nov. 7, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- A mismatch between two common tests for kidney function may indicate a higher risk for kidney failure, heart disease, and death, a new study shows. Health care providers for decades have measured blood levels of the molecule creatinine to track the rate at which kidneys filter waste from muscle breakdown in the bloodstream. According to more recent guidelines, levels of cystatin C, a small protein made by all cells in the body, can also be used to measure kidney function. Since these two tests are influenced by different factors — including some related to disease or aging — using both markers together can provide a better measure of kidney function and risk of organ failure than either one alone. Led by NYU Langone Health researchers, the new work reveals that many people, especially those who are sick, often have a large gap between the two readings, which may be a signal of future disease. Specifically, the global study shows that more than a third of hospitalized participants had a cystatin C-based readout of kidney function that was at least 30% lower than one based on their creatinine levels. Name: 10_larson_racing_newman_track
Representative_Docs: ["Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. The Joe Gibbs Racing star turned a 98.241-mph lap Saturday in the No. 11 Toyota to beat William Byron, whose No. 24 Chevrolet qualified second at 97.957 mph for Sunday’s 400-lap race. With his win two weeks ago at Las Vegas Motor Speedway, Hamlin, 45, broke a tie with Kevin Harvick for 10th on the all-time win list in NASCAR's premier series. He is two poles away from tying the career total of Ryan Newman, one of the greatest qualifiers in NASCAR history. “It’s pretty awesome,” said Hamlin, who grew up a few hours east of Martinsville in the Richmond, Virginia, suburb of Chesterfield. “Really with age, the hardest part is still having the fast time. It’s one thing to be able to manage races and use your experience to your advantage. But usually the first thing to go is your all-out speed, and we’re still knocking off poles, which is really good. I was around when Newman was just unstoppable in qualifying. So damn, I didn’t know he had that many.” Hamlin has six wins at Martinsville, and his vic", "Denny Hamlin qualifies first at Martinsville, moves into tie for 10th in NASCAR poles MARTINSVILLE, Va. — Denny Hamlin earned his fifth pole position at Martinsville Speedway and 49th of his Cup Series career, tying him with NASCAR Hall of Famer Bobby Isaac for 10th all time. Name: 11_surgical_surgeons_surgery_surgeries
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques.
